title: Robin Follows the History
description: On pages 8–11, this part follows Narrator, Unknown person #1, King Neptune, Fish-Man Island Citizens, Fish-Men, Hody Jones, New Fish-Man Pirates. The core story beat is robin follows the history.
scene: The scene centers on robin follows the history, with Narrator, Unknown person #1, King Neptune, Fish-Man Island Citizens, Fish-Men, Hody Jones, New Fish-Man Pirates driving the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This is what you'd call a "Graveyard of ships". Suken ships are brought here by the the the the the the the the the the flow of the currents.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ironic, isn't it? That this, of all places, would be blessed with the light of the suntree eve, and filled with beautiful coral...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even the fish and whales gather around it...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's the thing about whales...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They have a sense for the most plentiful areas of the ocean, and naturally gather there.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As for me, I'm living in these parts so I can research this mysterious ocean forest that has grown upon the ocean depths.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It certainly is hard to believe we're at the bottom of the ocean.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm impressed you managed to obtain wood from the treasure tree Adam.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And it is a wonderful vessel, worthy of that legendary wood.

dialogue.speaker: King Neptune
dialogue.text: You think so too, huh?! It's made of the very same materials Tom-san used to make the pirate King's ship!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And the design is my life's finest work!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hahah... I'd be glad to coat this ship for you. I'll handle it with a "don"!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's a real help!! uh... your name was...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The name's den!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Right. Den-san. Still, you really don't look much like him, huh...? I mean, you're a merman... and not even a puffer fish...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I thought, since you're Tom-san's brother, there'd be a little more resemblance, you know...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Elder Brother

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Younger brother

dialogue.speaker: King Neptune
dialogue.text: Hahahah. That's a very human way of thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I know most species that live above the ocean tend to have a heavy resemblance within families.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But it's a little different for us.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Fishmen and Merfolk carry a long, long memory of their heritage in their genes.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So for instance, if an octopus mermaid gives birth to a shark merman...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Parent and child

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That just means that somewhere way back on their family tree, they had a shark merman for an ancestor.

dialogue.speaker: Fish-Man Island Citizens
dialogue.text: On Fish-Man Island, it's practically impossible to predict what a child will look like just from looking at their parents.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So it's a little difficult for us to understand that human instinct...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...that leads you to classify people based on their appearances.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: By the by, you wouldn't happen to have...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...a robot 😉 As an Ancestor, would you?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'm a human, all right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I've heard all about you and iceburg-kun from those letters, you know.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh, right... old lady Kokoro was sending you letters...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So of course, I know all about the incident.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: my brother lived his life with a "don", right until the end...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And I'm happy to see that he had such a wonderful apprentice, who would come out of his way to let me know how it happened.

dialogue.speaker: King Neptune
dialogue.text: You're making me blush!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Bah!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...Hey, by the way, I've been wondering for a while now...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Who's that guy sitting over there?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh... that's the boss, jimbel.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Boss?

dialogue.speaker: Fish-Man Island Citizens
dialogue.text: apparently he's waiting for someone. He can't enter the island, of course, since he's a fugitive.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ENTRYWAY TO THE PALACE...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...the "Main Gate"...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...the "Palace Gates"...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You fiends...!! How did you make it all the way in here?!

dialogue.speaker: Vander Decken
dialogue.text: That face...!!! Why, you are the deplorable Vander Decken!!! Finally you show yourself!!

dialogue.speaker: Fish-Men
dialogue.text: And that man is...!! Hordy Jones, from the fishman district!!! What business do you have coming back here?!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But why is he...?!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: HORDY...?! that man is...

dialogue.speaker: King Neptune
dialogue.text: He was once... a skilled warrior in our very own Neptunee army...

summary: Robin Follows the History. This part advances the crew from their post-timeskip reunion into the undersea route and the first conflicts surrounding Fish-Man Island.
